The Geometry of Truth: How AI Spontaneously Learns to Separate Fact from Fiction | by NYU Center for Data Science | Medium

content.text:
Here are the results for your query:

## "Emergence of Linear Truth Encodings in Language Models"

This is a paper published at **NeurIPS 2025** (the 39th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems).

### Authors
Shauli Ravfogel, Gilad Yehudai, Tal Linzen, Joan Bruna, and Alberto Bietti
, from
New York University and the Flatiron Institute

.

### Key Findings
Recent probing studies reveal that large language models exhibit linear subspaces that separate true from false statements, yet the mechanism behind their emergence is unclear.
The authors introduce a transparent, one-layer transformer toy model that reproduces such truth subspaces end-to-end and exposes one concrete route by which they can arise.

They study one simple setting in which truth encoding can emerge: a data distribution where factual statements co-occur with other factual statements (and vice-versa), encouraging the model to learn this distinction in order to lower the LM loss on future tokens.
### Truth Co-occurrence Hypothesis (TCH)

The authors propose the Truth Co-occurrence Hypothesis (TCH): in naturally occurring text, true statements are statistically more likely to co-occur with other true statements, and falsehoods with other falsehoods.
### Two-Phase Learning Dynamic

In the toy setting, they observe a two-phase learning dynamic: networks first memorize individual factual associations in a few steps, then—over a longer horizon—learn to linearly separate true from false statements.
### Practical Implications
These findings have sparked interest among practitioners, because they may aid in mitigating hallucinations.
If engineers can identify the specific "direction" of truth within a model's internal geometry, they can potentially monitor when a model's generation drifts into the "false" region.

### Resources

- **arXiv**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.15804
- **Code**:
The authors released their code at https://github.com/shauli-ravfogel/truth-encoding-neurips
- **NeurIPS page**: https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/poster/117764
